plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-455 RP15.P02.M04.S07-RP15.P02.M06.S02 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-455 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP15.P02 workflow permission slice: the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail (the sixteen remaining rows), the full M05 Permission And Audit Binding cycle (twenty-two rows), and the M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head (the first two rows) as three descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H15), Claude review packet (C15), and CP00-456 handoff, without opening analytics, Matter P&L, forecast, or WIP runtime or any other closed runtime surface. CP00-455 also remediates the CP00-454 P3 finding by correcting the stale RP14 program label in the CP00-454 coverage test title.

parsed_review.summary:
CP00-455 is a descriptor-only closeout pack covering the RP15.P02 analytics workflow/permission slice (40 planned units, risk B: M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail x16, M05 Permission And Audit Binding x22, M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head x2). Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack is internally consistent and the production_ready posture is sound.

Verified: (1) Plan binding (closeout-pack-plan.json line 409), registry (ANALYTICS_CORE_CP455_PACK_BINDING/REQUIREMENTS), service descriptors, validators, index re-exports, test, contract (current_pack, p02_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor, cp455_requirements/no_write_attestation, historical_pack_bindings), and manifest all agree on pack_id, risk_class B, unit_count 40, range RP15.P02.M04.S07-RP15.P02.M06.S02, upstream CP00-454, next CP00-456/RP15.P02.M06.S03. Deliverable distribution (impl 16, ui 6, claude_review 2, failure_recovery 4, test 8, contract 2, security_audit 2) and micro-title distribution (16/22/2) both sum to 40 and match the included units. (2) All 28 commands in command-evidence.json passed (exit 0), incl. npm test, validate, rp15:analytics-core:validate, goal:closeout:validate, fullplan:validate, git diff --check. (3) No-write/no-real-data boundary holds: all changed source is pure functions returning Object.freeze descriptors; test/validator only read files; extensive frozen no-write/no-leak/synthetic-only attestations. (4) Claude stays read-only and non-final-approver (claude_is_final_approval/promotes_claude_to_final_approval false; human_final_approval_required true). (5) Hermes patterns are descriptor/evidence-only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product false). (6) production_ready is correctly false with status implemented_pending_review; the descriptor-only boundary is not weakened and no runtime is opened. (7) Review readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1436-byte valid-JSON stdout) — no forbidden review-evidence conditions (auth failure, 0-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields). (8) The claimed CP00-454 P3 remediation is applied — the CP00-454 coverage test title now reads RP15; remaining RP14 references are the legitimate upstream_program_id.

No P0/P1/P2 issues. One non-blocking P3: the CP455 descriptor-level no-leak/no-permission-eval guards are enforced by frozen constants but not asserted by an explicit test, unlike the row-level guards in CP453/CP454 (the difference is explained by row data shape, and the pattern matches the two already-accepted upstream packs). This pack does not block pack or goal closeout; final enterprise-trust approval remains with the human approver.

findings.title: Descriptor-level no-leak / no-permission-eval guards are frozen-constant-enforced but not test-asserted for the M05 Permission And Audit Binding slice
findings.details: validateAnalyticsCoreCp455P02WorkflowPermissionSliceDescriptor (lines 498-552) checks per-row descriptor_only/runtime_execution/customer_safe_errors_only/raw_payload_included plus the no_write_attestation and authority_boundary fields, but does not assert the result-level security guards that freezeCp455Result sets in service.js (lines 310-352): evaluates_runtime_permission:false, writes_audit_event:false, exposes_permission_decision_detail:false, exposes_audit_event_body:false, permission_bypass_detected:false, leak_detected:false. The CP00-455 test (test/model.test.js lines 201-217) likewise asserts only descriptor_only/runtime_execution and authority_boundary. Because CP455's M04/M05/M06 row titles do not match any ANALYTICS_CORE_CP455_ROW_EXTRAS key, every row resolves to empty extras, so there are no row-level leak sub-objects to validate the way CP453/CP454 do — the permission/audit no-leak posture for this slice rests entirely on the frozen result-level constants. These constants cannot drift at runtime (Object.freeze), and the manifest no-leak claims (no_permission_decision_detail_leak, no_runtime_permission_or_audit_evaluation, etc.) are therefore structurally true, but they are not protected by an explicit regression assertion. Given M05 is the Permission And Audit Binding micro carrying the two security_audit deliverables (Permission precheck, Audit hint precheck), this is the slice where such an assertion would have the most value. This is consistent with the already-accepted CP453/CP454 pattern and is not a regression.
findings.recommendation: Optional hardening for a future pack (does not block CP00-455): add a small assertion block to the CP00-455 descriptor test (or validator) confirming the descriptor result exposes evaluates_runtime_permission/writes_audit_event/exposes_permission_decision_detail/permission_bypass_detected/leak_detected as false, so the M05 permission-and-audit no-leak guarantees are guarded against future refactors that might unfreeze or restructure these objects.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking. Documented as optional defense-in-depth for a downstream pack. Posture is currently enforced by Object.freeze constants and matches the accepted CP453/CP454 convention; no change required for CP00-455 closeout.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-455 analytics core p02 workflow permission slice descriptor pack
